title: Spawn Now
line1: Adds random live cells for this layer's current behavior.
line2: Behavior-specific Spawn Now rows describe the exact shape or entity.

title: Spawn Now
line1: Seeds random active cells for Brian's Brain.
line2: This shares the same Spawn Now intent across behaviors.

title: Spawn Now
line1: Adds an ant to the current grid.
line2: This shares the same Spawn Now intent across behaviors.

title: Spawn Now
line1: Adds a ball to the simulation.
line2: This shares the same Spawn Now intent across behaviors.

title: Spawn Now
line1: Spawns a pulse shape.
line2: This shares the same Spawn Now intent across behaviors.

title: Spawn Now
line1: Drops a new raindrop immediately.
line2: This shares the same Spawn Now intent across behaviors.

title: Spawn Now
line1: Seeds a new DLA cluster.
line2: This shares the same Spawn Now intent across behaviors.

title: Spawn Now
line1: Spawns a glider pattern.
line2: This shares the same Spawn Now intent across behaviors.

title: Save Current
line1: Saves changes to the currently loaded preset file.
line2: If no preset is loaded, this action is unavailable.

title: Refresh List
line1: Refreshes preset names from storage.
line2: Use this if files changed outside the app.

path: System > Saves > Library > Save As > Save
title: Save As
line1: Saves the current configuration to the name you entered.
line2: If the name exists, confirmation is required.

title: Load Preset
line1: Loads the selected preset and replaces current runtime configuration.
line2: Use Save Current or Save As first if you want to keep edits.

title: Delete Preset
line1: Deletes the selected preset from storage.
line2: This action asks for confirmation.

title: Rename Preset
line1: Chooses the preset that will be renamed.
line2: Then enter a new name and apply.

title: MIDI Enabled
line1: Turns MIDI features on or off.
line2: Disable if you want the engine to run without MIDI I/O.

title: Panic
line1: Sends all-notes-off style panic to clear stuck notes.
line2: Use this if external devices hang notes.

title: MIDI Out Disconnect
line1: Disconnects the current MIDI output target.
line2: No MIDI output port will be selected.

title: MIDI In Disconnect
line1: Disconnects the current MIDI input source.
line2: No MIDI input port will be selected.

title: Select MIDI Output
line1: Selects this MIDI output device for note/event transmission.
line2: Use Disconnect to clear selection.

title: Select MIDI Input
line1: Selects this MIDI input device for transport/sync control.
line2: Use Disconnect to clear selection.

path: System > MIDI > Sync & Clock > Sync Mode
title: Sync Mode
line1: Chooses whether timing is internal or external MIDI clock.
line2: External mode follows incoming clock when enabled.

path: System > MIDI > Sync & Clock > Clock Out
title: Clock Out
line1: Enables outgoing MIDI clock messages.
line2: Useful when other gear should follow this transport.

path: System > MIDI > Sync & Clock > Clock In
title: Clock In
line1: Enables incoming MIDI clock processing.
line2: Required to follow external timing.

path: System > MIDI > Sync & Clock > Follow Start/Stop
title: Follow Start/Stop
line1: When enabled, external MIDI Start/Stop controls transport.
line2: When disabled, transport reacts only to local controls.

title: Worlds
line1: Configures each layer's grid behavior and step timing.
line2: Shape the cell process here before Pulses turns activity into notes.

title: Step Rate
line1: Sets how often the Life algorithm advances per musical time unit.
line2: Faster rates evolve patterns quickly; slower rates make longer-form motion.

title: Behavior
line1: Selects the current layer's Life behavior engine.
line2: Changing behavior resets that layer's live grid state to the new defaults.

title: Pulses
line1: Controls how grid activity is interpreted into trigger intents.
line2: This is where scan/event sources get routed toward instruments.

title: Layer
line1: Configures the selected layer's behavior, timing, and runtime settings.
line2: Each of the 8 layers runs its own behavior and Worlds/Pulses config independently.

title: Layer
line1: Per-layer Pulses interpretation, scanning, and trigger routing settings.
line2: Organized into submenus including Instrument Targets, Trigger Prob., Note Mapping, and X/Y axis modulation.

title: Behavior
line1: Selects which behavior engine owns this layer's grid and runtime state.
line2: Switching behavior replaces the layer's current behavior state with that behavior's defaults.

title: Step Rate
line1: Sets how often this layer's behavior advances relative to transport time.
line2: Use faster rates for dense motion and slower rates for long-form pattern change.

title: Spawn Count
line1: Sets how many random cells are injected by this layer's spawn-style behavior action.
line2: Higher values create denser bursts and faster pattern disruption.

title: Spawn Interval
line1: Sets how many behavior ticks pass between automatic random spawn bursts.
line2: Lower values create more frequent injections.

title: Glider Interval
line1: Sets how many behavior ticks pass between automatic glider injections.
line2: Use 0 to disable automatic glider spawning.

title: Spawn Step
line1: Sets the sequenced position or interval used by behaviors that step their own spawn source.
line2: Increase it to spread spawned changes farther apart in time or space.

title: Punch In/Out
line1: Toggles the looper between overdub and play.
line2: Keeps the recorded loop and current playback position.

title: Length
line1: Sets the loop length in this layer's behavior steps.
line2: Use a faster Step Rate for tighter recording and playback resolution.

title: Clear Loop
line1: Clears the recorded looper sequence for this layer.
line2: Live-held cells stay active; loop playback cells are released.

title: Save Grid State
line1: When On, this layer's runtime/grid snapshot is stored in preset and default saves.
line2: Turn it Off if you want to keep configuration but not the current live cell state.

title: Auto Label
line1: When On, the layer label follows the active behavior or generated default label.
line2: Turn it Off before typing a custom layer label you want to keep.

title: Layer Label
line1: Sets the user-visible label for this layer.
line2: Used across menus and overlays when Auto Label is off.

title: Save Grid State
line1: When On, this layer's runtime/grid state is stored in preset/default saves.
line2: When Off, saves keep this layer's config but not its current runtime/grid snapshot.

title: Scan Mode
line1: Options: none, scanning. none uses whole-grid interpretation without a scan cursor.
line2: scanning moves a cursor by scan unit, axis, and direction and emits scanned/scanned-empty triggers.

title: Scan Axis
line1: Sets whether scanning traverses rows or columns.
line2: Combine with Scan Direction to define scan movement.

title: Scan Unit
line1: Sets scan cursor advance rate in musical units.
line2: Smaller units move faster; larger units move slower.

title: Scan Direction
line1: Sets scan travel direction along the selected axis.
line2: Use reverse for back-and-forth style phrasing with manual resets.

title: Sections
line1: Splits scanning into 1, 2, 4, or 8 lane sections.
line2: 1 preserves current full-grid scan behavior; higher values scan smaller lanes in sequence.

title: Event Triggers
line1: Enables transition triggers such as activate, deactivate, and stable for this layer.
line2: Turn it Off to suppress event-based note generation while keeping other Pulses settings intact.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > Events > Instrument Targets
title: Instrument Targets
line1: Maps each trigger kind to an action and destination instrument slot.
line2: Use note_off actions for release-style lifecycle behavior.

title: Activate Action
line1: Defines what happens when a cell becomes active.
line2: Default note_on starts notes on birth, spawn, or press transitions.

title: Activate Instrument
line1: Selects which instrument slot receives activate events.
line2: Point this at the slot that should sound when a cell turns on.

title: Stable Action
line1: Defines behavior for cells that stay active across updates.
line2: Default none avoids constant retriggering from unchanged active cells.

title: Stable Instrument
line1: Selects destination instrument slot for stable triggers.
line2: Use with stable action when you want sustained-state gestures.

title: Deactivate Action
line1: Defines what happens when a cell turns inactive.
line2: Default note_off releases matching notes in lifecycle workflows.

title: Deactivate Instrument
line1: Selects destination instrument slot for deactivate triggers.
line2: Typically points to the same slot used by activate note_on.

title: Scanned Action
line1: Defines behavior when scan encounters a filled cell.
line2: Default note_on makes scanning immediately audible.

title: Scanned Instrument
line1: Selects destination instrument slot for scanned-filled triggers.
line2: Use different slots for layered scan and event voicing.

title: Scanned Empty Action
line1: Defines behavior when scan encounters an empty cell.
line2: Default note_off can release held scan notes.

title: Scanned Empty Instrument
line1: Selects destination instrument slot for scanned-empty triggers.
line2: Pair with the scanned slot for directional hold-and-release patterns.

title: Tones
line1: Defines pitch mapping and instrument slot sound engines.
line2: This layer controls how sensed events become audible voices.

title: Note Mapping
line1: Sets pitch quantization, range, and root/scale behavior.
line2: Applied before per-instrument synth shaping.

title: Starting Note
line1: Sets the reference note before X/Y pitch step offsets are applied for this layer.
line2: Use this as the tonal center anchor.

title: Lowest Note
line1: Sets the lower bound for generated notes.
line2: Notes outside range are handled by Out of Range mode.

title: Highest Note
line1: Sets the upper bound for generated notes.
line2: Together with Lowest Note this defines the playable range.

title: Out of Range
line1: Chooses how pitch indices beyond bounds are handled.
line2: Clamp pins to edges; wrap folds back through the range.

title: Scale
line1: Selects scale intervals used for pitch quantization.
line2: Use this to set harmonic language for the layer.

title: Root
line1: Sets the tonic/root for selected scale quantization.
line2: Changing root transposes quantized output harmonically.

title: Instruments
line1: Holds the destination instrument slots that Pulses trigger mappings play into.
line2: Each slot can run synth, sampler, or MIDI settings with its own mixer and naming controls.

title: Instrument MIDI
line1: Controls external MIDI note output for this slot.
line2: Enable it and choose the outgoing channel.

title: Instrument Slot
line1: Configures one destination instrument slot.
line2: Pulses mappings route trigger actions into these slots.

title: Instrument Slot
line1: Configures one destination instrument slot.
line2: Pulses mappings route trigger actions into these slots.

title: Instrument Slot
line1: Configures one destination instrument slot.
line2: Pulses mappings route trigger actions into these slots.

title: Instrument Slot
line1: Configures one destination instrument slot.
line2: Pulses mappings route trigger actions into these slots.

title: Instrument Slot
line1: Configures one destination instrument slot.
line2: Pulses mappings route trigger actions into these slots.

title: Instrument Slot
line1: Configures one destination instrument slot.
line2: Pulses mappings route trigger actions into these slots.

title: Instrument Slot
line1: Configures one destination instrument slot.
line2: Pulses mappings route trigger actions into these slots.

title: Instrument Slot
line1: Configures one destination instrument slot.
line2: Pulses mappings route trigger actions into these slots.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Synth > Osc 1
title: Oscillator 1
line1: Controls first oscillator voice.
line2: Use with Oscillator 2 for layered tone shaping.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Synth > Osc 2
title: Oscillator 2
line1: Controls second oscillator voice.
line2: Detune/octave offsets create width and harmonic spread.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Synth > Volume
title: Volume
line1: Amplitude and loudness contour controls.
line2: Use Amp and Envelope to define dynamics.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Synth > Amp Env
title: Amp Envelope
line1: ADSR loudness contour for the slot.
line2: Shapes attack, sustain body, and release tail.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Synth > Filter
title: Filter
line1: Frequency-shaping section for this slot.
line2: Use cutoff/resonance and filter envelope for movement.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Synth > Filter Env
title: Filter Envelope
line1: ADSR contour that modulates filter movement.
line2: Works together with Env Amount.

title: Instrument Type
line1: Selects this slot's engine: silent, internal synth, sampler playback, or external MIDI output.
line2: The editable section below changes to match the selected type.

title: Instrument Type
line1: Selects this slot's engine: silent, internal synth, sampler playback, or external MIDI output.
line2: The editable section below changes to match the selected type.

title: Instrument Type
line1: Selects this slot's engine: silent, internal synth, sampler playback, or external MIDI output.
line2: The editable section below changes to match the selected type.

title: Instrument Type
line1: Selects this slot's engine: silent, internal synth, sampler playback, or external MIDI output.
line2: The editable section below changes to match the selected type.

title: Instrument Type
line1: Selects this slot's engine: silent, internal synth, sampler playback, or external MIDI output.
line2: The editable section below changes to match the selected type.

title: Instrument Type
line1: Selects this slot's engine: silent, internal synth, sampler playback, or external MIDI output.
line2: The editable section below changes to match the selected type.

title: Instrument Type
line1: Selects this slot's engine: silent, internal synth, sampler playback, or external MIDI output.
line2: The editable section below changes to match the selected type.

title: Instrument Type
line1: Selects this slot's engine: silent, internal synth, sampler playback, or external MIDI output.
line2: The editable section below changes to match the selected type.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > Sample Slot
title: Sample Slot
line1: Selects which one of the 8 sample slots is currently focused.
line2: Choose Sample and Assign actions apply to the selected slot.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > S* Browse
title: Choose Sample
line1: Browses wav files under the `samples/` folder.
line2: Space previews the highlighted file through this sampler slot's mixer route.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > Assign
title: Assign
line1: Enters grid assignment mode for the selected sample slot.
line2: Back exits; Shift+cell applies to row, Fn+Shift+cell applies to column.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > Velocity Levels
title: Velocity Levels
line1: When on, assigned cells store High/Medium/Low level states.
line2: Pressing a selected-slot cell cycles Off -> High -> Medium -> Low -> Off.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > Velocity Levels > Level High
title: Level High
line1: Base velocity used for High assignment level.
line2: Final playback velocity is this value scaled by Pulses/global velocity shaping.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > Velocity Levels > Level Medium
title: Level Medium
line1: Base velocity used for Medium assignment level.
line2: Final playback velocity is this value scaled by Pulses/global velocity shaping.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > Velocity Levels > Level Low
title: Level Low
line1: Base velocity used for Low assignment level.
line2: Final playback velocity is this value scaled by Pulses/global velocity shaping.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > Base Velocity
title: Base Velocity
line1: Used when Velocity Levels are off.
line2: Assigned cells then play as binary on/off with this base velocity.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > S* Browse > (loading...)
title: Loading Samples
line1: Shows while the current sample folder is loading.
line2: Wait for folders, wav files, or `(empty)` to appear.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > S* Browse > ..
title: Browse Up
line1: Moves to the parent folder within `samples/`.
line2: Cannot navigate outside the `samples/` root.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > S* Browse > *
title: Enter Folder
line1: Opens the highlighted bracketed folder.
line2: Navigation stays inside the `samples/` folder tree.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > S* Browse > *
title: Pick Sample
line1: Assigns the highlighted wav to the selected sample slot.
line2: The slot path updates immediately.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > S* Browse > Set favourite
title: Set Favourite
line1: Adds the current folder to the sample favourites list.
line2: The action applies to the open folder, not the highlighted file.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > S* Browse > Remove favourite
title: Remove Favourite
line1: Removes the current folder from the sample favourites list.
line2: The action applies to the open folder, not the highlighted file.

title: Scanning
line1: Controls scan cursor movement, subdivision, and cursor-based instrument targets.
line2: Scan mode, axis, unit, direction, sections; plus action/instrument mappings for scanned-filled and scanned-empty.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > Scanning > Instrument Targets
title: Scan Instrument Targets
line1: Maps scan cursor hits (filled and empty) to action and instrument slot.
line2: Action defaults to none so you opt into scan-based triggering.

title: Events
line1: Controls transition event triggering and event-based instrument targets.
line2: Event Triggers toggle, plus activate/stable/deactivate action/instrument mappings.

title: Pulses Note Mapping
line1: Per-layer pitch mapping used during Pulses interpretation and instrument routing.
line2: Defines starting note, range, scale, and root before events reach instrument slots.

title: Trigger Prob.
line1: Controls saved trigger probability for this layer.
line2: Mode, low/high values, and the probability grid decide which triggers pass.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > Trigger Prob. > Mode
title: Trigger Probability Mode
line1: zero blocks this layer, custom uses the grid, full passes all triggers.
line2: Sparks Trigger Gate can override live playback without changing this saved mode.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > Trigger Prob. > Low Prob
title: Low Probability
line1: Sets the low custom trigger probability percentage from 0 to 100.
line2: Clamped so it never exceeds High Prob.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > Trigger Prob. > High Prob
title: High Probability
line1: Sets the high custom trigger probability percentage from 0 to 100.
line2: Clamped so it is never below Low Prob.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > Trigger Prob. > Map Probability Grid
title: Map Probability Grid
line1: Edits the saved per-cell probability map for this layer.
line2: Cells cycle zero, low, high, full; Shift applies rows and Shift+Fn columns.

title: Mappings
line1: Explicit menu-based assignment for Pulses X/Y param-mod slots and aux encoder turn targets.
line2: These pickers reuse the same parameter browser as Sparks X/Y target selection.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > Mappings > X Axis
title: X Axis Targets
line1: Holds the two explicit X-axis param-mod slot assignments for this layer.
line2: Use Slot 1 and Slot 2 to pick targets, then use the invert toggles to reverse each lane.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > Mappings > Y Axis
title: Y Axis Targets
line1: Holds the two explicit Y-axis param-mod slot assignments for this layer.
line2: Use Slot 1 and Slot 2 to pick targets, then use the invert toggles to reverse each lane.

title: Aux Mappings
line1: Assigns each aux encoder's Turn target and Switch/press action.
line2: Hold Fn for alternate aux overlay: labels without `!` are turn targets and `!Label` means the press action.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > Aux Mappings > Auto Map
title: Auto Map
line1: Turns context-sensitive aux auto-mapping on or off.
line2: When enabled, the current menu context can temporarily supply aux targets; custom Fn+Aux alternate bindings still take priority.

title: Aux Mapping
line1: Per-encoder binding submenu for one aux encoder.
line2: Turn stores the rotary target; Click stores the press action shown with `!` in the Fn alternate aux overlay.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > Aux Mappings > Aux * > Turn
title: Aux Turn
line1: Selects which parameter this aux encoder turn controls.
line2: You can also focus a bindable value row and press Fn+Aux as an alternate action to store it as that encoder's custom Turn binding.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > Aux Mappings > Aux * > Click
title: Aux Click
line1: Selects which action this aux encoder press triggers.
line2: You can also focus a bindable action row and press Fn+Aux as an alternate action to store it as that encoder's custom `!` press binding.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > Aux Mappings > Aux * > Click > Layers
title: Layers
line1: Lists layers whose current behavior exposes bindable click actions.
line2: Open a layer to bind one of its current behavior actions to the selected aux click.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > Aux Mappings > Aux * > Click > Layers > L*
title: Layer Actions
line1: Lists the currently bindable behavior actions for this layer's active behavior.
line2: These are the same actions available through the current behavior's menu actions.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > Aux Mappings > Aux * > Click > Sample Assign
title: Sample Assign
line1: Binds aux click to open sample-slot grid assignment for a sampler instrument.
line2: Only sampler instruments appear here.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > Aux Mappings > Aux * > Click > FX Map
title: FX Map
line1: Binds aux click to enter FX map-to-grid assignment using the current selected FX config.
line2: This stores the selected FX snapshot at bind time.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > Mappings > X Axis > Slot *
title: X Slot
line1: Selects which parameter this X-axis modulation slot controls.
line2: The browser is shared with Sparks X/Y target selection so available targets stay consistent.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > Mappings > Y Axis > Slot *
title: Y Slot
line1: Selects which parameter this Y-axis modulation slot controls.
line2: The browser is shared with Sparks X/Y target selection so available targets stay consistent.

title: Clear Slot Target
line1: Clears the selected Pulses param-mod slot so that lane no longer writes any parameter.
line2: Existing invert state is preserved if you later assign a new target.

title: Clear Aux Turn
line1: Clears the selected aux encoder turn binding.
line2: Any existing aux click binding for that encoder is preserved.

title: Clear Aux Click
line1: Clears the selected aux encoder click binding.
line2: Any existing aux turn binding for that encoder is preserved.

title: Slot 1 Invert
line1: When On, X Slot 1 writes the selected parameter using an inverted lane direction.
line2: Low positions become high values and vice versa.

title: Slot 2 Invert
line1: When On, X Slot 2 writes the selected parameter using an inverted lane direction.
line2: Low positions become high values and vice versa.

title: Slot 1 Invert
line1: When On, Y Slot 1 writes the selected parameter using an inverted lane direction.
line2: Low positions become high values and vice versa.

title: Slot 2 Invert
line1: When On, Y Slot 2 writes the selected parameter using an inverted lane direction.
line2: Low positions become high values and vice versa.

title: Note Mode
line1: Controls retrigger versus hold behavior for this instrument slot.
line2: Use hold for lifecycle note_off workflows and scanned-empty release.

title: MIDI Enabled
line1: Enables this slot's external MIDI note output.
line2: Global MIDI and an output port must also be enabled.

title: MIDI Channel
line1: Selects this slot's external MIDI channel.
line2: Shown as 1..16 while stored internally as 0..15.

title: MIDI Velocity
line1: Sets the fixed velocity for external MIDI notes from this slot.
line2: Use when Pulses should drive pitch and timing only.

title: MIDI Duration
line1: Sets the default length for this slot's external MIDI notes.
line2: Applies to oneshot note behavior.

title: Oscillator Wave
line1: Selects the oscillator waveform shape.
line2: Use different waves per osc for richer timbre blends.

title: Oscillator Level
line1: Sets this oscillator's contribution to the voice mix.
line2: Balance osc1/osc2 levels to shape tone body.

title: Oscillator Octave
line1: Offsets oscillator pitch in octave steps.
line2: Useful for layered bass/body/air intervals.

title: Oscillator Detune
line1: Offsets oscillator pitch by cents for beating/width.
line2: Small detune values create motion without changing harmony.

title: Pulse Width
line1: Sets pulse duty cycle when pulse waveform is used.
line2: Ignored for non-pulse waveforms.

title: Amp Gain
line1: Sets base loudness for this instrument slot.
line2: Use with Master Vol to balance global versus per-slot level.

title: Velocity Sensitivity
line1: Controls how strongly note velocity affects loudness.
line2: Lower values flatten dynamics; higher values increase response.

title: Amp Envelope
line1: Shapes loudness over time using ADSR stages.
line2: Attack/Decay/Release are in ms; Sustain is level percentage.

title: Filter Type
line1: Selects the filter response mode for this slot.
line2: Use alongside cutoff/resonance and filter envelope depth.

title: Filter Cutoff
line1: Sets base cutoff frequency for tone brightness.
line2: Higher values pass more high content.

title: Filter Resonance
line1: Boosts emphasis near cutoff frequency.
line2: High resonance can create pronounced tonal peaks.

title: Filter Env Amount
line1: Sets how much filter envelope modulates cutoff.
line2: Positive values open with envelope; negative values invert.

title: Filter Key Tracking
line1: Sets how cutoff follows played pitch across notes.
line2: Higher values keep timbre more consistent by pitch.

title: Filter Envelope
line1: Shapes filter movement over time using ADSR stages.
line2: Use with Env Amount for plucks, swells, and contour motion.

title: BPM
line1: Sets master tempo in beats per minute.
line2: Affects internal clock rate and algorithm pulse timing.

title: Sound
line1: Global volume, voice shaping, and note behavior controls.
line2: Includes Master Vol, Voice Limit, Note Length, Velocity Scale, Velocity Curve, and Output Buffer.

title: Master Volume
line1: Sets overall output volume scaling.
line2: Use this for final level trim without changing patch balance.

title: Voice Limit
line1: Controls voice stealing: fixed12, fixed16, auto-soft, auto-balanced, auto-hard, or none.
line2: auto-hard keeps more CPU headroom; auto-soft preserves longer tails.

title: Output Buffer
line1: Sets Pi audio output buffer frames for the next device restart.
line2: Smaller values reduce latency; larger values improve stability. OCTESSERA_AUDIO_OUTPUT_BUFFER_FRAMES still overrides this at startup.

title: Presets
line1: Save, load, and manage configuration snapshots.
line2: Use defaults for boot state and library for named variants.

title: Save Default
line1: Stores current state as startup default payload.
line2: Use with Auto Save when you want edits to persist automatically.

title: Load Default
line1: Loads the saved default payload into current state.
line2: Replaces current runtime settings immediately.

title: Shutdown
line1: Shows the shutdown splash, then closes the simulator or powers off the Pi gracefully.
line2: Requires confirmation before shutting down.

title: Reboot
line1: Shows the shutdown splash, then reboots the Pi gracefully.
line2: Requires confirmation before rebooting.

title: Auto Save Default
line1: When enabled, config edits update default storage after a short cooldown so sweeps save only the latest settled value.
line2: Turning this on and exiting the row saves the current config immediately.

title: Backups
line1: When enabled, config edits write rolling backup files at most every five minutes.
line2: The host keeps the latest 20 backups and writes a recovery save during shutdown or reboot.

title: Note Length
line1: Default note duration when note_on has no explicit length.
line2: Hold-mode notes may bypass duration and wait for note_off.

title: Velocity Scale
line1: Scales velocity values globally after mapping.
line2: Use to tame or boost overall dynamic output.

title: UI
line1: Display and lighting behavior for the device/simulator UI.
line2: These do not change musical generation.

title: Basic Help
line1: Opens a quick reference for core hardware gestures.
line2: Includes help, back, transport, Fn navigation, alternate aux binding, sample, and probability-map shortcuts.

title: Ghost Cells
line1: Shows dim cells from inactive layers behind the active layer.
line2: Off by default for clarity; active layer cells always take visual priority.

title: Events when paused
line1: When On, grid presses continue to fire musical events even when transport is stopped or paused.
line2: Disable to keep paused/stopped grid edits silent while algorithm evolution remains stopped.

title: Dim Timer
line1: Sets idle timeout before non-OLED LEDs dim.
line2: Set 0 to keep LEDs at configured brightness.

title: OLED Sleep
line1: Sets idle timeout before OLED sleep.
line2: Set 0 to keep display always on.

title: OLED Bright
line1: Sets OLED brightness level.
line2: Higher values improve visibility at cost of brightness.

title: Grid Bright
line1: Sets grid LED brightness scaling.
line2: Lower values are useful for dark environments.

title: Button Bright
line1: Sets NeoKey/button LED brightness scaling.
line2: Helps match hardware feel to ambient light.

title: Number Style
line1: Controls how bar-style numeric parameters are displayed.
line2: Choose bar only, numbers only, or bar plus numbers.

title: Auto Map
line1: Enables context-sensitive aux mappings for the active menu row.
line2: When off, only custom aux bindings are used and auto-map prefixes disappear.

title: X Axis Modulation
line1: Maps X position into pitch/velocity/filter modulation lanes.
line2: Use offsets and ranges to reshape horizontal influence.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > X Axis > Pitch Steps
title: X Pitch Steps
line1: Controls pitch stepping derived from X position.
line2: Disable to remove X-axis pitch influence.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > X Axis > Velocity
title: X Velocity Lane
line1: Maps X position to velocity output range.
line2: Use this for left-right dynamic shaping.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > X Axis > Filter Cutoff
title: X Cutoff Lane
line1: Maps X position to CC74 cutoff modulation range.
line2: Works when filter cutoff lane is enabled.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > X Axis > Filter Resonance
title: X Resonance Lane
line1: Maps X position to CC71 resonance modulation range.
line2: Use carefully to avoid excessive resonance peaks.

title: Y Axis Modulation
line1: Maps Y position into pitch/velocity/filter modulation lanes.
line2: Use alongside X axis for two-dimensional mapping.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > Y Axis > Pitch Steps
title: Y Pitch Steps
line1: Controls pitch stepping derived from Y position.
line2: Disable to remove Y-axis pitch influence.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > Y Axis > Velocity
title: Y Velocity Lane
line1: Maps Y position to velocity output range.
line2: Useful for top-bottom accent patterns.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > Y Axis > Filter Cutoff
title: Y Cutoff Lane
line1: Maps Y position to CC74 cutoff modulation range.
line2: Combine with X lane for richer filter motion.

path: Menu > 2: Pulses > L*: * > Y Axis > Filter Resonance
title: Y Resonance Lane
line1: Maps Y position to CC71 resonance modulation range.
line2: Lower ranges are usually easier to control musically.

title: X Pitch Enabled
line1: Enables pitch stepping derived from X position.
line2: Disable to remove horizontal pitch influence for this layer.

title: X Pitch Steps
line1: Sets semitone-step influence per X grid step.
line2: Larger values create wider horizontal intervals.

title: Restart Section
line1: When On, X pitch stepping restarts from the lane origin in each scan section.
line2: Turn it Off to keep pitch indexing continuous across section boundaries.

title: X Velocity Enabled
line1: Enables velocity modulation from X position.
line2: Disable for fixed mapped velocity from the trigger target.

title: X Cutoff Enabled
line1: Enables cutoff CC modulation from X position.
line2: Outputs CC74 when this lane is active.

title: X Resonance Enabled
line1: Enables resonance CC modulation from X position.
line2: Outputs CC71 when this lane is active.

title: Y Pitch Enabled
line1: Enables pitch stepping derived from Y position.
line2: Disable to remove vertical pitch influence for this layer.

title: Y Pitch Steps
line1: Sets semitone-step influence per Y grid step.
line2: Larger values create wider vertical intervals.

title: Restart Section
line1: When On, Y pitch stepping restarts from the lane origin in each scan section.
line2: Turn it Off to keep pitch indexing continuous across section boundaries.

title: Y Velocity Enabled
line1: Enables velocity modulation from Y position.
line2: Disable for fixed mapped velocity from the trigger target.

title: Y Cutoff Enabled
line1: Enables cutoff CC modulation from Y position.
line2: Outputs CC74 when this lane is active.

title: Y Resonance Enabled
line1: Enables resonance CC modulation from Y position.
line2: Outputs CC71 when this lane is active.

title: Tones X Axis
line1: Alternate X-axis modulation settings at Tones layer.
line2: Use this when you want different mapping emphasis in Tones context.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > X Axis > Pitch Steps
title: Tones X Pitch Steps
line1: Controls X-based pitch stepping in Tones layer.
line2: Can diverge from Pulses defaults for performance tuning.

title: Tones X Velocity Lane
line1: Controls X-based velocity modulation in Tones layer.
line2: Use to rebalance loudness response per performance profile.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > X Axis > Filter Cutoff
title: Tones X Cutoff Lane
line1: Controls X-based cutoff modulation in Tones layer.
line2: Affects generated CC74 values.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > X Axis > Filter Resonance
title: Tones X Resonance Lane
line1: Controls X-based resonance modulation in Tones layer.
line2: Affects generated CC71 values.

title: Tones Y Axis
line1: Alternate Y-axis modulation settings at Tones layer.
line2: Use for per-context modulation tuning.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Y Axis > Pitch Steps
title: Tones Y Pitch Steps
line1: Controls Y-based pitch stepping in Tones layer.
line2: Higher steps create larger vertical interval jumps.

title: Tones Y Velocity Lane
line1: Controls Y-based velocity modulation in Tones layer.
line2: Use to weight top/bottom cell dynamics.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Y Axis > Filter Cutoff
title: Tones Y Cutoff Lane
line1: Controls Y-based cutoff modulation in Tones layer.
line2: Affects generated CC74 values.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Y Axis > Filter Resonance
title: Tones Y Resonance Lane
line1: Controls Y-based resonance modulation in Tones layer.
line2: Affects generated CC71 values.

title: System
line1: Global configuration area for presets, sound, MIDI, and UI.
line2: Use this for environment and persistence behavior.

title: Diagnostics
line1: Hardware checks before the Pi runtime starts.
line2: Use Hardware Test to verify boot config, pins, and DAC detection.

title: Hardware Test
line1: Runs the Pi pre-hardware diagnostic checks.
line2: Requires confirmation before running the guided test.

title: Updates
line1: Update status and OTA control actions.
line2: Check is read-only; Apply and Rollback confirm first.

title: Check
line1: Checks whether an update is available.
line2: This is a non-destructive status request.

title: Apply
line1: Applies the staged update package.
line2: Requires confirmation before changing the active release.

title: Rollback
line1: Restores the previous release.
line2: Requires confirmation before switching versions.

title: Preset Library
line1: Named save/load/rename/delete workflow for patch states.
line2: Use Refresh if external files were changed.

path: Menu > System > Saves > Library > Save As
title: Save As
line1: Creates a preset from the current runtime state.
line2: Enter a name then run Save to write it.

title: Load Preset
line1: Lists available presets to load into current state.
line2: Loading replaces current runtime config.

title: Rename Preset
line1: Select a preset then edit new name before applying.
line2: Useful for versioning variants.

title: Delete Preset
line1: Select a preset to delete from storage.
line2: Deletion requires confirmation.

title: Default Preset
line1: Controls boot/default payload save and load behavior.
line2: Auto Save can keep default synced with edits.

title: Factory Preset
line1: Loads built-in factory baseline configuration.
line2: Use this as a safe recovery starting point.

title: MIDI
line1: Global MIDI routing, ports, and clock behavior.
line2: Per-instrument MIDI enable/channel are in 3: Tones > Instruments.

title: MIDI Output
line1: Selects target output device for outgoing MIDI events.
line2: Disconnect clears the selected output.

title: MIDI Input
line1: Selects input device for incoming clock/start/stop.
line2: Disconnect disables input source selection.

title: Sync & Clock
line1: Configures transport clock source and clock I/O behavior.
line2: Set external mode to follow incoming MIDI clock.

title: Spawn Count
line1: Sets how many random cells are injected per spawn event.
line2: Higher values create denser, more chaotic growth bursts.

title: Spawn Interval
line1: Sets tick interval between automatic random spawn bursts.
line2: Lower values spawn more frequently.

title: X Pitch Enabled
line1: Enables pitch stepping derived from X position.
line2: Disable to remove horizontal pitch influence.

title: X Pitch Steps
line1: Sets semitone-step influence per X grid step.
line2: Larger values create wider horizontal intervals.

title: X Velocity Enabled
line1: Enables velocity modulation from X position.
line2: Disable for fixed mapped velocity from trigger target.

title: X Cutoff Enabled
line1: Enables cutoff CC modulation from X position.
line2: Outputs CC74 when enabled.

title: X Resonance Enabled
line1: Enables resonance CC modulation from X position.
line2: Outputs CC71 when enabled.

title: Y Pitch Enabled
line1: Enables pitch stepping derived from Y position.
line2: Disable to remove vertical pitch influence.

title: Y Pitch Steps
line1: Sets semitone-step influence per Y grid step.
line2: Larger values create wider vertical intervals.

title: Y Velocity Enabled
line1: Enables velocity modulation from Y position.
line2: Disable for fixed mapped velocity from trigger target.

title: Y Cutoff Enabled
line1: Enables cutoff CC modulation from Y position.
line2: Outputs CC74 when enabled.

title: Y Resonance Enabled
line1: Enables resonance CC modulation from Y position.
line2: Outputs CC71 when enabled.

title: Preset Name Field
line1: Text field used for Save As and Rename preset flows.
line2: Save/Apply actions consume this value as the target preset name.

path: Menu > System > Saves > Library > Rename > Apply
title: Apply Rename
line1: Runs rename from selected preset to entered New Name.
line2: Requires a valid selected source preset.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Slot Actions
title: Instrument Actions
line1: Rare destructive or copy actions for this instrument slot.
line2: Clone or reset only after confirming the target action.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Slot Actions > Clone
title: Clone
line1: Copies this instrument slot's configuration into another instrument slot.
line2: Use this to duplicate a sound before making variations.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Slot Actions > Reset
title: Reset
line1: Restores this instrument slot to its default state for the current type.
line2: Useful when you want to clear experimental edits on one slot only.

title: Auto Label
line1: When On, the instrument label follows its current type or generated label.
line2: Turn it Off before typing a custom instrument label you want to keep.

title: Instrument Name
line1: Sets the user-visible label for this instrument slot.
line2: Shown in menus, routing targets, and overlays when Auto Label is off.

title: Auto Label
line1: When On, the FX bus label follows its generated default label.
line2: Turn it Off before typing a custom bus label you want to keep.

title: Bus Name
line1: Sets the user-visible label for this FX bus.
line2: Shown in routing menus and bus-related overlays when Auto Label is off.

title: Velocity Curve
line1: Selects the global curve used to remap velocity response after scaling.
line2: Use gentler curves for flatter dynamics or steeper curves for more accent contrast.

path: Menu > System > Saves > Factory > Load Factory
title: Load Factory
line1: Loads built-in factory default configuration.
line2: Use this to quickly recover a known baseline state.

title: Synth Preset
line1: Preset tools for this instrument slot's synth parameters.
line2: Use Load to replace the slot's synth block with a named preset.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Preset > Load
title: Load Synth Preset
line1: Lists available synth presets for this instrument slot.
line2: Selecting a preset asks for confirmation before overwriting synth settings.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Preset > Load > *
title: Load Synth Preset
line1: Loads the chosen synth preset into this instrument slot.
line2: A confirmation step protects against accidental overwrite.

title: Instrument Mixer
line1: Sets this slot's audio level, route, and pan.
line2: Mixer settings affect synth and sampler audio, not external MIDI notes.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Mixer > Route
title: Route
line1: Sends this slot's synth or sampler audio direct or to one FX bus.
line2: External MIDI notes bypass audio routing and FX.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Mixer > Pan Pos
title: Pan Position
line1: Sets direct-output pan for this slot's audio.
line2: 0 is left, 16 center, 32 right; bus-routed audio uses the bus pan.

title: FX Buses
line1: Configures shared audio buses used by instrument routes.
line2: Each bus runs Slot 1 then Slot 2, pans the result, and joins the main mix.

title: Bus Names
line1: Sets auto-naming and custom name for each bus.
line2: Auto name is computed from the assigned FX slot types; turning off auto lets you edit the name freely.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > FX Buses > Names > Bus *
title: Bus Name
line1: Configures naming for this bus.
line2: Auto Label on derives a label from FX slot types; Name lets you type any text when Auto is off.

title: FX Bus
line1: Builds one shared bus chain for any instruments routed to this bus.
line2: Slot 1 feeds Slot 2, then Pan Pos places the bus in the stereo main mix.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > FX Buses > B*: * > Slot 1
title: FX Slot 1
line1: First effect slot in the bus chain.
line2: Default None means passthrough.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > FX Buses > B*: * > Slot 2
title: FX Slot 2
line1: Second effect slot in the bus chain.
line2: Default None means passthrough.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > FX Buses > B*: * > Pan Pos
title: FX Bus Pan Position
line1: Sets where this bus output sits in the stereo mix.
line2: 0 is left, 16 center, 32 right.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > FX Buses > B*: * > Slot 1
title: FX Slot
line1: Configures one effect slot in the bus chain.
line2: Choose Type, then edit effect parameters below.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > FX Buses > B*: * > Slot 2
title: FX Slot
line1: Configures one effect slot in the bus chain.
line2: Choose Type, then edit effect parameters below.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > FX Buses > B*: * > Slot 1 > Type
title: Slot Type
line1: Selects the first bus effect, or none for passthrough.
line2: Choosing an effect initializes its editable parameter defaults.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > FX Buses > B*: * > Slot 2 > Type
title: Slot Type
line1: Selects the second bus effect, or none for passthrough.
line2: Choosing an effect initializes its editable parameter defaults.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > FX Buses > B*: * > Slot 1 > duck
title: duck
line1: Sidechain-style ducking effect.
line2: Reduces this bus level when the selected source is loud.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > FX Buses > B*: * > Slot 2 > duck
title: duck
line1: Sidechain-style ducking effect.
line2: Reduces this bus level when the selected source is loud.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > FX Buses > B*: * > Slot 1 > duck > Source
title: duck Source
line1: Selects which signal triggers ducking.
line2: Options are I# (instrument) and B# (bus), capability-sized.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > FX Buses > B*: * > Slot 2 > duck > Source
title: duck Source
line1: Selects which signal triggers ducking.
line2: Options are I# (instrument) and B# (bus), capability-sized.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > FX Buses > B*: * > Slot 1 > compressor
title: compressor
line1: Dynamics processor that reduces gain when signal exceeds a threshold.
line2: Adjust threshold, ratio, attack, release, makeup gain, and dry/wet mix.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > FX Buses > B*: * > Slot 2 > compressor
title: compressor
line1: Dynamics processor that reduces gain when signal exceeds a threshold.
line2: Adjust threshold, ratio, attack, release, makeup gain, and dry/wet mix.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > FX Buses > B*: * > Slot 1 > eq
title: eq
line1: Three-band equalizer with low shelf, peaking mid, and high shelf bands.
line2: Boosts or cuts each band independently; gain range ±12 dB.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > FX Buses > B*: * > Slot 2 > eq
title: eq
line1: Three-band equalizer with low shelf, peaking mid, and high shelf bands.
line2: Boosts or cuts each band independently; gain range ±12 dB.

title: Global FX
line1: Processes the full stereo main mix after instruments and FX buses are summed together.
line2: These slots run before momentary Sparks FX and Master Vol, so they color the whole output.

title: Global FX Slot
line1: Configures one stereo master effect slot.
line2: Choose Type, then edit the parameters shown below.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Global FX > Slot * > Type
title: Slot Type
line1: Options: none, vinyl, eq, compressor, saturator, distortion.
line2: Selecting an effect initializes its editable parameter defaults; none is passthrough.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Global FX > Slot * > vinyl
title: vinyl
line1: Lo-fi stereo master effect with warm saturation, crackle, and shared warp movement.
line2: Designed as a lighter-weight master color effect for current Pi Zero targets.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Global FX > Slot * > compressor
title: compressor
line1: Stereo-linked dynamics processor for the summed main mix.
line2: Threshold, ratio, attack, release, makeup gain, and dry/wet mix affect both channels together.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Global FX > Slot * > eq
title: eq
line1: Stereo three-band equalizer for the summed main mix.
line2: Use it for broad tonal shaping after instrument and bus routing.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Global FX > Slot * > saturator
title: saturator
line1: Soft-clipping saturation applied to the stereo main mix.
line2: Useful for gentle density and warmth without adding extra routing.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Global FX > Slot * > distortion
title: distortion
line1: Harder clipping/shaping applied to the stereo main mix.
line2: Use sparingly; aggressive settings can dominate the full output.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Mixer > Volume
title: Mixer Volume
line1: Sets the per-instrument post-voice fader from 0 to 100.
line2: The Sparks mix page also controls this value from the grid.

title: Sparks
line1: Performance grid layer for live mix, pan, FX, Trigger Gate, and X/Y Pad pages.
line2: Hold Fn for navigation: left column selects layer; right column selects Sparks page. Release Fn to keep performing on the selected layer.

title: Mix Page
line1: Live grid page for per-layer mix gestures.
line2: Fn navigation with the right grid column also selects Sparks pages for performance.

title: Pan Page
line1: Live grid page for per-layer pan gestures.
line2: Fn navigation with the right grid column also selects Sparks pages for performance.

title: FX Page
line1: Configures and performs momentary Sparks FX.
line2: Fn navigation with the right grid column also selects Sparks pages for performance.

title: Trigger Gate Page
line1: Live grid page for trigger probability gate controls.
line2: Fn navigation with the right grid column also selects Sparks pages for performance.

title: Transpose Page
line1: Live grid page for transient synth/MIDI note transposition.
line2: Left column selects layers; piano cells choose semitone offsets.

title: XY Page
line1: Live grid page for X/Y parameter modulation.
line2: Fn navigation with the right grid column also selects Sparks pages for performance.

title: Mix Page
line1: Live grid page for per-layer mix gestures.
line2: Fn navigation with the right grid column also selects Sparks pages for performance.

title: Pan Page
line1: Live grid page for per-layer pan gestures.
line2: Fn navigation with the right grid column also selects Sparks pages for performance.

title: FX Page
line1: Configures and performs momentary Sparks FX.
line2: Fn navigation with the right grid column also selects Sparks pages for performance.

title: Trigger Gate Page
line1: Live grid page for trigger probability gate controls.
line2: Fn navigation with the right grid column also selects Sparks pages for performance.

title: Transpose Page
line1: Live grid page for transient synth/MIDI note transposition.
line2: Left column selects layers; piano cells choose semitone offsets.

title: XY Page
line1: Live grid page for X/Y parameter modulation.
line2: Fn navigation with the right grid column also selects Sparks pages for performance.

title: Aux Map
line1: Shows the Sparks FX parameters and actions that are auto-mapped to aux controls.
line2: Rows keep the same 1- and 1! OLED markers as live auto-mapped aux controls; values can be edited here, but mappings are not changed.

title: BPM
line1: Sets master tempo in beats per minute.
line2: This is the main tempo control.

title: Swing
line1: Delays the off-beat internal step feel while keeping the main transport clock stable.
line2: 0% is straight timing. Higher values add more groove to internal triggers and scan movement.

title: FX Type
line1: Selects the momentary Sparks effect: none, stutter, freeze, filter sweep, or pitch shift.
line2: Changing type seeds that effect's default parameters for grid mapping.

title: Rate Hz
line1: Sets the captured segment repeat rate (1-32 Hz). Higher rate = shorter captured segment.
line2: Stored per grid cell when Map to Grid is used.

title: Depth
line1: Sets stutter wet mix from 0 (dry) to 100 (fully wet repeat) for the config being mapped.
line2: Stored per grid cell when Map to Grid is used.

title: Release Ms
line1: Sets freeze release fade time in milliseconds (10-5000). Controls how long the reverb tail fades out after releasing the grid cell.
line2: Stored per grid cell when Map to Grid is used.

title: Mix
line1: Sets wet mix from 0 to 100 for freeze or pitch_shift configs being mapped.
line2: Stored per grid cell when Map to Grid is used.

title: Cutoff
line1: Sets filter_sweep target cutoff amount from 0 (higher cutoff) to 100 (lower cutoff). Sweeps from fully open on press toward this target.
line2: Stored per grid cell when Map to Grid is used.

title: Res
line1: Sets filter_sweep resonance amount from 0 to 100 for the config being mapped.
line2: Stored per grid cell when Map to Grid is used.

title: Sweep In
line1: Sets filter_sweep sweep-in time in milliseconds (10-3000). Duration to sweep from open filter toward the target cutoff on press.
line2: Stored per grid cell when Map to Grid is used.

title: Sweep Out
line1: Sets filter_sweep sweep-out time in milliseconds (10-3000). Duration to sweep from target back to fully open filter on release.
line2: Stored per grid cell when Map to Grid is used.

title: Semitones
line1: Sets pitch_shift semitone offset (-24..24) for the config being mapped. Combined with Cents for fine tuning.
line2: Stored per grid cell when Map to Grid is used.

title: Cents
line1: Sets pitch_shift cents offset (-100..100) for fine detuning the config being mapped. Added to Semitones for the total shift.
line2: Stored per grid cell when Map to Grid is used.

title: Behavior
line1: Opens the behavior browser for this layer.
line2: Choose a category, then choose a behavior. Selection updates only this layer's Worlds rows and keeps the saved behaviorId payload field.

title: Behavior
line1: Selects the behavior engine for this layer.
line2: Compatibility entry for older generated or fixture rows that still expose behavior as an enum.

title: Step Rate
line1: Sets how often this layer's behavior advances relative to transport timing.
line2: Use faster rates for denser motion and slower rates for longer phrases.

title: Spawn Count
line1: Sets how many cells or entities are injected by this behavior's spawn action.
line2: Higher values make spawn actions denser and more disruptive.

title: Spawn Interval
line1: Sets the tick interval between automatic random spawn bursts.
line2: Lower values spawn more frequently.

title: Reset Behavior
line1: Reinitializes the selected layer's active behavior state.
line2: Configuration stays in place while the live grid/runtime state is reset.

title: Select Behavior
line1: Selects this behavior for the active layer.
line2: The runtime switches behavior immediately through the native engine without a full menu rebuild.

title: Back
line1: Returns to the parent browser level.
line2: Use it like the hardware Back button when browsing synthetic folders.

title: Bind Parameter
line1: Assigns this parameter to the selected modulation, XY, or aux target slot.
line2: The target stores this concrete parameter key and value metadata.

title: Clear Binding
line1: Clears the selected modulation, XY, or aux target binding.
line2: The target slot becomes unassigned.

title: MIDI Enabled
line1: Turns MIDI integration on or off for the runtime.
line2: When off, MIDI port selections and external event output are ignored.

title: Sync Mode
line1: Chooses whether transport follows internal timing or external MIDI clock.
line2: External mode uses incoming realtime MIDI messages when Clock In is enabled.

title: State Notes
line1: Enables state-derived notes during transport ticks for this layer.
line2: Turn it off to keep input events without continuous state playback.

title: X Lane Curve
line1: Selects the response curve for this X-axis value lane.
line2: linear maps evenly; curve stores the curved response mode for modulation parity.

title: Y Lane Curve
line1: Selects the response curve for this Y-axis value lane.
line2: linear maps evenly; curve stores the curved response mode for modulation parity.

title: X Lane From
line1: Sets the low endpoint for this X-axis value lane.
line2: The lane scales grid position between From and To.

title: X Lane To
line1: Sets the high endpoint for this X-axis value lane.
line2: The lane scales grid position between From and To.

title: X Grid Offset
line1: Rotates the X-axis lane index before scaling values.
line2: Useful for shifting where low and high modulation points fall on the grid.

title: Y Lane From
line1: Sets the low endpoint for this Y-axis value lane.
line2: The lane scales grid position between From and To.

title: Y Lane To
line1: Sets the high endpoint for this Y-axis value lane.
line2: The lane scales grid position between From and To.

title: Y Grid Offset
line1: Rotates the Y-axis lane index before scaling values.
line2: Useful for shifting where low and high modulation points fall on the grid.

title: Sampler
line1: Configures sample-slot playback, grid assignment, velocity levels, filter, and amp shaping.
line2: Visible for sampler instruments.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > Velocity Levels
title: Velocity Levels
line1: Configures High, Medium, and Low velocities for assigned sampler cells.
line2: Visible when Velocity Levels is on.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > Amp Env
title: Amp Envelope
line1: Shapes sampler loudness over time with ADSR stages.
line2: Attack/Decay/Release are milliseconds; Sustain is percentage.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Sampler > Filter Env
title: Filter Envelope
line1: Shapes sampler filter motion over time with ADSR stages.
line2: Use with filter Env Amount for plucks and sweeps.

title: Tune Semis
line1: Transposes sampler playback in semitones.
line2: Negative values tune down; positive values tune up.

title: Sample Gain
line1: Sets base sampler loudness for this instrument slot.
line2: Use with mixer volume and master volume for level balance.

title: Sample Velocity Sensitivity
line1: Controls how strongly trigger velocity affects sampler loudness.
line2: Lower values flatten dynamics; higher values increase response.

title: Sample Amp Envelope
line1: Shapes sampler loudness over time using ADSR stages.
line2: Attack/Decay/Release are in ms; Sustain is level percentage.

title: Sample Filter Type
line1: Selects the sampler filter response mode.
line2: Use with cutoff, resonance, envelope amount, and key tracking.

title: Sample Filter Cutoff
line1: Sets the sampler filter cutoff on the compact 0..255 control scale.
line2: Higher values pass more high content.

title: Sample Filter Resonance
line1: Boosts emphasis near the sampler filter cutoff.
line2: High values create stronger peaks.

title: Sample Filter Env Amount
line1: Sets how much the sampler filter envelope moves cutoff.
line2: Positive values open with envelope; negative values invert.

title: Sample Filter Key Tracking
line1: Sets how sampler filter cutoff follows played pitch.
line2: Higher values keep timbre more consistent across pitch.

title: Sample Filter Envelope
line1: Shapes sampler filter movement over time using ADSR stages.
line2: Use with Env Amount for plucks, swells, and contour motion.

title: Instrument Type
line1: Selects whether this slot is silent, synth, sampler, or external MIDI.
line2: Only controls relevant to the selected type are shown.

title: MIDI Velocity
line1: Sets fixed outgoing note velocity for this MIDI instrument slot.
line2: Use when Pulses should drive pitch/timing but not velocity dynamics.

title: MIDI Duration
line1: Sets default outgoing MIDI note length in milliseconds.
line2: Useful for shaping external MIDI staccato or sustain.

title: Synth
line1: Configures the internal two-oscillator synth for this slot.
line2: Visible for synth instruments.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Synth > Amp Env
title: Amp Envelope
line1: Shapes synth loudness over time with ADSR stages.
line2: Attack/Decay/Release are milliseconds; Sustain is percentage.

path: Menu > 3: Tones > Instruments > Instrument * > Synth > Filter Env
title: Filter Envelope
line1: Shapes synth filter motion over time with ADSR stages.
line2: Use with filter Env Amount for plucks and sweeps.

title: Invert X
line1: Flips the Sparks XY X-axis modulation direction.
line2: Left becomes high and right becomes low when enabled.

title: Invert Y
line1: Flips the Sparks XY Y-axis modulation direction.
line2: Bottom becomes high and top becomes low when enabled.

title: XY Release
line1: Chooses what happens when a Sparks XY touch is released.
line2: sample-hold keeps the last position; reset-center returns to center.

title: FX Target
line1: Chooses where the mapped Sparks FX cell is inserted.
line2: Targets include master, FX buses, and instruments.

title: XY Axis Target
line1: Chooses a parameter target for this Sparks XY axis.
line2: The picker uses the shared parameter browser.

title: Behavior Parameters
line1: Lists behavior parameters available for binding.
line2: Selecting a row binds that parameter to the current target slot.

title: Instrument Parameters
line1: Lists instrument parameters available for binding.
line2: Selecting a row binds that parameter to the current target slot.

title: Instrument Slot Parameters
line1: Lists bindable parameters for this instrument slot.
line2: Only active parameter families should be used at performance time.

title: Sample Parameters
line1: Lists bindable sampler parameters for this instrument slot.
line2: Selecting a row binds that parameter to the current target slot.

title: Synth Parameters
line1: Lists bindable synth parameters for this instrument slot.
line2: Selecting a row binds that parameter to the current target slot.

title: Sound Parameters
line1: Lists global sound parameters available for binding.
line2: Selecting a row binds that parameter to the current target slot.

title: Duck Amount
line1: Sets how strongly ducking reduces the target bus.
line2: Higher percentages create deeper ducking.

title: Attack
line1: Sets how quickly this effect reacts after input crosses its trigger condition.
line2: Short values respond faster.

title: Release
line1: Sets how quickly this effect returns after release or input falls away.
line2: Longer values decay more slowly.

title: Threshold
line1: Sets the normalized trigger level for this effect.
line2: Lower values trigger more easily.

title: Threshold dB
line1: Sets compressor threshold in dB.
line2: Signals above this level are reduced.

title: Mix
line1: Sets wet/dry mix for this effect.
line2: 0 is dry; 100 is fully wet.

title: Delay Time
line1: Sets delay time in milliseconds.
line2: Longer values create wider echoes.

title: Feedback
line1: Sets how much output feeds back into the effect.
line2: Higher values increase repeats or resonance.

title: Rate Hz
line1: Sets modulation rate in Hertz.
line2: Higher rates move faster.

title: Depth
line1: Sets modulation depth as a percentage.
line2: Higher values create stronger movement.

title: Depth ms
line1: Sets modulation depth in milliseconds.
line2: Used by chorus, flanger, and vibrato style effects.

title: Base ms
line1: Sets the base delay time in milliseconds.
line2: Depth is applied around this base.

title: Drive
line1: Sets nonlinear drive amount.
line2: Higher values add more saturation or distortion.

title: Clip
line1: Sets distortion clipping threshold.
line2: Lower values clip harder.

title: Bits
line1: Sets bit depth for bitcrushing.
line2: Lower values sound rougher.

title: Rate Div
line1: Sets sample-rate reduction divisor.
line2: Higher values reduce more aggressively.

title: Center Hz
line1: Sets the center frequency for filter modulation.
line2: This anchors the sweep range.

title: Q
line1: Sets filter resonance/Q.
line2: Higher values emphasize the cutoff region.

title: Decay
line1: Sets reverb feedback decay.
line2: Displayed as approximate tail time.

title: Damp
line1: Sets reverb high-frequency damping.
line2: Higher values darken the tail faster.

title: Chance
line1: Sets glitch trigger probability.
line2: Higher values glitch more often.

title: Slice ms
line1: Sets glitch slice length.
line2: Shorter slices sound tighter and more fragmented.

title: Ratio
line1: Sets compressor ratio.
line2: Higher ratios reduce peaks more strongly.

title: Makeup dB
line1: Adds gain after compression.
line2: Use it to recover level after gain reduction.

title: Low Gain dB
line1: Adjusts low-band EQ gain.
line2: Negative cuts lows; positive boosts lows.

title: Mid Gain dB
line1: Adjusts mid-band EQ gain.
line2: Negative cuts mids; positive boosts mids.

title: High Gain dB
line1: Adjusts high-band EQ gain.
line2: Negative cuts highs; positive boosts highs.

title: Mid Frequency
line1: Sets the EQ mid-band center frequency.
line2: Move it to target different tonal ranges.

title: Mid Q
line1: Sets the EQ mid-band width.
line2: Higher values make a narrower band.

title: Saturation
line1: Sets vinyl saturation amount.
line2: Higher values add more warmth and compression.

title: Crackle
line1: Sets vinyl crackle amount.
line2: Higher values add more noise events.

title: Warp Depth
line1: Sets vinyl pitch/time warp depth.
line2: Higher values add more wobble.

title: Threshold dB
line1: Sets compressor threshold in dB.
line2: Signals above this level are reduced.

title: Ratio
line1: Sets compressor ratio.
line2: Higher ratios reduce peaks more strongly.

title: Makeup dB
line1: Adds gain after compression.
line2: Use it to recover level after gain reduction.

title: Low Gain dB
line1: Adjusts low-band EQ gain.
line2: Negative cuts lows; positive boosts lows.

title: Mid Gain dB
line1: Adjusts mid-band EQ gain.
line2: Negative cuts mids; positive boosts mids.

title: High Gain dB
line1: Adjusts high-band EQ gain.
line2: Negative cuts highs; positive boosts highs.

title: Mid Frequency
line1: Sets the EQ mid-band center frequency.
line2: Move it to target different tonal ranges.

title: Mid Q
line1: Sets the EQ mid-band width.
line2: Higher values make a narrower band.

title: Clip
line1: Sets distortion clipping threshold.
line2: Lower values clip harder.

title: Drive
line1: Sets nonlinear drive amount.
line2: Higher values add more saturation or distortion.

title: Mix
line1: Sets wet/dry mix for this effect.
line2: 0 is dry; 100 is fully wet.

title: Rate Hz
line1: Sets modulation rate in Hertz.
line2: Higher rates move faster.

title: Attack
line1: Sets how quickly this global effect reacts.
line2: Short values respond faster.

title: Release
line1: Sets how quickly this global effect returns.
line2: Longer values decay more slowly.

path: Menu > 4: Sparks > FX > Map to Grid
title: Map to Grid
line1: Enters FX assignment mode using the current effect type and values.
line2: Press a grid cell to store it; Back exits assignment mode.

path: Menu > 4: Sparks > FX > Aux Map > Map to Grid
title: Map to Grid
line1: Enters FX assignment mode using the current Sparks FX settings.
line2: The row uses the same auto-map press marker as the aux encoder action and does not remap the aux control.

title: X Axis
line1: Selects which parameter the X axis modulates. Options include the same mappable parameters available for aux encoders and X/Y Pulses modulation.
line2: Choose (none) to disable X axis modulation.

title: Y Axis
line1: Selects which parameter the Y axis modulates. Options include the same mappable parameters available for aux encoders and X/Y Pulses modulation.
line2: Choose (none) to disable Y axis modulation.

title: Release Behavior
line1: Controls what happens when the grid finger is lifted.
line2: sample-hold keeps the last modulation value; reset-center returns X and Y to center (0.5, 0.5).

title: Invert X
line1: Flips the X axis value.
line2: When on, left becomes maximum and right becomes minimum.

title: Invert Y
line1: Flips the Y axis value.
line2: When on, bottom becomes maximum and top becomes minimum.
